body:
QThread-based recording engine that captures LMU shared memory telemetry and writes compressed `.tmu` files. Implements the recorder (issue #4) along with its dependencies: binary serializer (#2), LZ4 compression (#3), and the `.tmu` format definitions (#1).

### New modules

- **`tmu_format.py`** — `.tmu` binary file format: 40B header (magic, version, offsets), JSON session metadata, channel definition table, LZ4-compressed frame chunks, chunk index table for seekability, 24B footer
- **`tmu_serializer.py`** — Maps 47 channels from `LMUVehicleTelemetry`/`LMUVehicleScoring` to flat binary frames via `struct.pack`. Handles K→°C, velocity→km/h, and other unit conversions. Supports configurable channel subsets.
- **`tmu_compression.py`** — `StreamingCompressor` accumulates N frames per chunk, compresses with `lz4.frame` (level 0 for minimal latency), maintains chunk index for seeking
- **`telemetry_recorder.py`** — `TelemetryRecorder(QThread)`:
  - Configurable sample rate: 10/20/30/60 Hz
  - Thread-safe ring buffer (`collections.deque` + `QMutex`) decouples polling from disk I/O
  - `start_recording(path)` / `stop_recording()` / `pause_recording()` / `resume_recording()`
  - Signals: `recording_started`, `recording_stopped`, `error(str)`, `stats_updated(dict)`
  - On stop: drains ring buffer → flushes compressor → writes chunk index table → writes footer

### Usage

```python
recorder = TelemetryRecorder(sample_rate=60)
recorder.recording_started.connect(on_started)
recorder.stats_updated.connect(on_stats)
recorder.start_recording("/path/to/session.tmu")
# ...
recorder.stop_recording()  # finalizes file with index table + footer
```

### Other changes
- Added `lz4>=4.3.0` to `pyproject.toml`
- Added `__pycache__/` and `*.pyc` to `.gitignore`
- 20 unit tests covering format round-trips, compression, serialization, and end-to-end file write/read

> <issue_description>## Description
> Core recording engine that captures shared memory data and writes compressed `.tmu` files.
> 
> ## Requirements
> - QThread-based recorder running alongside TelemetryReader
> - Configurable sample rate (10Hz, 20Hz, 30Hz, 60Hz)
> - Ring buffer to decouple reading from disk writes
> - Proper file finalization on stop (flush buffers, write index table, close)
> - Thread-safe start/stop/pause/resume API
> - Emit signals: recording_started, recording_stopped, error, stats_updated
> 
> ## Depends On
> - Binary serializer (#2)
> - LZ4 compression (#3)</issue_description>

commits.messageBody:
- Add tmu_format.py: .tmu file format (header, channel table, chunk index, footer)
- Add tmu_serializer.py: binary serializer mapping LMU shared memory to channels
- Add tmu_compression.py: LZ4 streaming compression with chunk indexing
- Add telemetry_recorder.py: QThread-based recorder with ring buffer,
  configurable sample rate, thread-safe start/stop/pause/resume API,
  and recording_started/recording_stopped/error/stats_updated signals
- Add lz4 dependency to pyproject.toml
- Add unit tests for all components (20 tests passing)

Co-authored-by: SeamusMullan <43112447+SeamusMullan@users.noreply.github.com>
commits.messageHeadline: Implement TelemetryRecorder with binary serializer and LZ4 compression
